title: Finite Hard-Margin SVM

solver_reuse.target: finite hard-margin SVM bad bias QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite replay computes the maximum-margin hyperplane bias -1 from the committed support vectors, while a malformed row claims -1/2, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict beneath the KKT and duality-gap replay.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional SVM rows only when they add distinct soft-margin/hinge-loss, kernel, working-set/SMO, geometric-margin, statistical-generalization, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep strong duality, KKT sufficiency theorems, margin-based generalization bounds, and floating-point training claims in theorem or statistical-inference lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite training set, the committed maximum-margin weights and bias, the dual multipliers, every functional margin, the support-vector margin equalities, the KKT stationarity/balance/complementary-slackness identities, and the zero primal-dual gap as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false bias claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting bias equation.
Expose the pack through statistics, optimization, linear-algebra, SVM, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep strong duality and KKT sufficiency theorems, geometric margins, soft-margin and kernel variants, solver/SMO behavior, statistical generalization, and floating-point training marked theorem, statistical-inference, or numerical-honesty horizon.
